hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point spike to 70% smells like deal momentum, probably shorts covering ahead of a framework announcement or backroom progress leaking to the right people. At nearly 7-in-10 odds, the market is pricing in a shutdown that either never happens or ends fast, but a March 31 deadline gives Congress two full budget cycles to punt again, which is their favorite move.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Resolution Suddenly Got 10 Points Cheaper, Here's Why
daily_take.deck: The DHS shutdown odds jumped 10.1 points to 69.8% in 24 hours on fresh signals that Congress might actually strike a deal before the March 31 deadline. But this market is pricing in baseline competence from politicians, which is a sucker's bet. Smart money should wait for actual legislative text, not hope.
